mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future earnings and signals policy support for risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is already easing, because the market interprets cuts as pure accommodation rather than a reaction to renewed price pressure.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and lower household income growth, which depresses equity valuations and forward returns over the next month. The signal should be negative when unemployment is rising faster than the economy can absorb through payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods deliver positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign flips positive after controlling for market-wide drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in INDPRO indicates improving industrial capacity utilization and expected earnings revisions for cyclical firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial production improves only because of temporary reopening effects without broad demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d SPX returns do not occur more often than negative returns after rising INDPRO prints, or if the 2-year rolling IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS after a prior contraction signals improving consumer and business spending, lifting near-term corporate revenue expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when retail sales growth exceeds payroll growth, indicating real demand normalization rather than inflation alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive after 2 consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS reversals are followed by negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the signal is indistinguishable from random over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises while RSAFS also expands, the economy is moving from supply-constrained recovery toward broad domestic demand strength, which supports equity multiples and earnings expectations over the next quarter. The interaction matters because housing alone can be noisy, but housing plus retail sales confirms a synchronized demand rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-RSAFS improvements do not outperform either series alone, or if triggered joint-strength periods fail to produce positive 60d SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL inflation implies policy remains easy relative to inflation, which compresses real rates and supports risk assets over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation accelerates enough to force imminent tightening expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate/disinflation episodes do not show positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the interaction term has a negative out-of-sample coefficient over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals labor-market stress that reduces consumption expectations and increases recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This short-horizon effect should be strongest during abrupt labor-market deterioration rather than slow-moving cycles.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if monthly labor deterioration events are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the average 5d return is nonnegative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A positive gap between INDPRO recovery and weak HOUST growth indicates production is normalizing faster than housing and broader final demand, which signals incomplete cyclical healing and negative equity returns over the next quarter. This regime favors caution because supply-side improvement without housing confirmation often precedes demand disappointment.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-led recoveries without HOUST confirmation still produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction effect is not significant in rolling walk-forward tests.
